steps.title: Compute the confidence interval
steps.content: A margin of error of $0.2$ ounce around a sample mean of $10.83$ ounces gives the interval $[10.83 - 0.2,\ 10.83 + 0.2] = [10.63,\ 11.03]$ ounces. Plausible values for the true population mean lie within this range.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — between 10.63 and 11.03 ounces: correctly describes values inside the interval $\pm$ the margin of error. ✓
  • Choice B — less than 10.63 or greater than 11.03 ounces: describes values outside the interval — the opposite of what the margin of error tells us. ✗
  • Choice C — greater than 10.98 ounces: $10.98 = 10.83 + 0.15$ has no meaning here; the margin of error defines a two-sided interval, not a one-sided lower bound. ✗
  • Choice D — less than 10.98 ounces: same flaw as C — uses a one-sided bound that does not follow from the margin of error. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
